coquetry.def: 媚態、賣弄風騷的人物 (單數) (嬌)
coquetry.ex: She used all her soft coquetry on him.

coquettish.ex: She gave him a very coquettish smile.

coquettishly.ex: She laughed coquettishly at his joke.

coral.def: 珊瑚、珊瑚色的、珊瑚蟲的人的人人物 (單數) (珊)
coral.col: coral reef, coral island, coral pink
coral.ex: The bright coral reef is full of fish.

corbel.def: (建)托架、聚樂的人的人人物 (單數)
corbel.ex: A stone corbel supports the local roof.

cord.def: 細繩、電線、(醫)臍帶的人物 (單數) (繩)
cord.col: vocal cords, power cord, spinal cord, umbilical cord
cord.ex: Please plug the power cord into the wall.

cordage.ex: The ship used some very heavy cordage.

cordate.ex: The plant has a beautiful cordate leaf.

cordial.def: 熱誠的、衷心的、甜酒人物 (單數) (熱)
cordial.ex: They gave us a very warm and cordial welcome.

cordiality.ex: He showed great cordiality to the guests.

cordially.ex: You are cordially invited to our wedding.

cordon.def: 警戒線、飾帶、圍住的人的人人物 (單數) (戒)
cordon.ex: Police managed to cordon off the area.

cordon-bleu.def: 一流水準的(烹飪)、藍帶的人物 (單數)
cordon-bleu.ex: She is a famed cordon-bleu chef today.

corduroy.def: 燈芯絨、燈芯絨褲的人物 (單數)
corduroy.ex: He wore a pair of brown corduroy pants.

core.def: 核心、心、果核、最重要部分的人物 (單數) (核)
core.col: hard core, core value, core curriculum, apple core
core.ex: Honesty is a core value of this firm.

corespondent.def: (律)共同被告、通姦者人物 (單數)
corespondent.ex: He was named corespondent in the divorce.

corgi.ex: The Welsh corgi ran around the garden.

coriander.ex: Add fresh coriander to the spicy soup.

cork.def: 軟木塞、軟木的人的人人物 (單數) (塞)
cork.ex: He pulled the wine cork out with a pop.

corker.def: (口)極好的事、極佳的人物 (單數)
corker.ex: The party last night was a real corker.

corking.def: (口)極好的、頂呱呱的人的人物
corking.ex: We had a corking time at the beach.

corkscrew.def: 螺旋起子、螺旋狀的人的人人物 (單數) (螺旋)
corkscrew.ex: The path followed a long corkscrew shape.

corm.ex: He planted the lily corm in the garden.

cormorant.def: 鸕鶿、貪婪的人人物 (單數)
cormorant.ex: The black cormorant dived for a fish.

corn.def: 玉米、穀物、雞眼、陳詞濫調的人物 (單數) (粟)
corn.col: corn flake, sweet corn, candy corn
corn.ex: He loves eating sweet corn in summer.

cornball.def: 鄉巴佬、陳腐的事人物 (單數)
cornball.ex: The movie was a real cornball story.

corncrake.ex: The shy corncrake hid in long grass.

cornea.ex: The doctor examined his damaged cornea.

corner.def: 角落、困境、轉角、壟斷的人的人人物 (單數) (角)
corner.col: around the corner, cut corners, fight in a corner
corner.ex: Spring is just around the corner now.

cornerstone.def: 奠基石、基礎人物 (單數) (基)
cornerstone.ex: Trust is the cornerstone of marriage.

cornet.def: (樂)短號、圓錐形蛋捲人物 (單數)
cornet.ex: He plays the cornet in the local band.

cornice.def: 簷口、線腳、雪簷的人物 (單數)
cornice.ex: A frozen cornice hung from the roof.

cornstalk.ex: The tall cornstalk swayed in the wind.

cornucopia.def: 豐饒之角、豐富的人物 (單數) (豐)
cornucopia.ex: The harvest was a real cornucopia of food.

corny.def: 陳腐的、玉米的人的人物 (舊)
corny.ex: He told a very long and corny joke.

corolla.ex: The bright corolla attracted many bees.

corollary.def: 推論、必然結果的人物 (單數)
corollary.ex: Success is a natural corollary of effort.

corona.def: 日冕、光環、冠狀物人物 (單數) (冠)
corona.ex: The solar corona is visible during eclipse.

coronary.def: 冠狀的、冠狀動脈的人的人物 (單數)
coronary.ex: He suffered a serious coronary attack.

coronation.def: 加冕、加冕典禮人物 (單數) (冕)
coronation.ex: The royal coronation was a grand event.

coroner.ex: The court coroner examined the body today.

coronet.ex: The princess wore a small silver coronet.

corporal.def: 肉體的、(軍)下士的人的人物 (單數) (體)
corporal.ex: Corporal punishment is banned in schools.

corporate.def: 公司的、法人的、共同的人的人物 (公)
corporate.col: corporate identity, corporate ladder, corporate world
corporate.ex: He is climbing the local corporate ladder.

corporately.ex: The board decided corporately to sell firm.

corporation.def: 公司、法人、大肚子的人物 (單數) (公)
corporation.ex: Toyota is a massive global corporation.

corporeal.def: 肉體的、物質的人的人物
corporeal.ex: The ghost had no corporeal form at all.

corps.def: (軍)軍團、隊、專業團體人物 (單數) (隊)
corps.col: press corps, peace corps, army corps
corps.ex: A large press corps followed the leader.

corpse.ex: The rotting corpse was found in the river.

corpulence.ex: His state of corpulence made walking hard.

corpulent.ex: The man was rather corpulent and slow.

corpus.def: 全集、資料庫、(解)體、本金人物 (單數) (集)
corpus.ex: The scholar studied the whole corpus of work.

corpuscle.def: 微粒、(生)血球人物 (單數) (粒)
corpuscle.ex: The red corpuscle carries oxygen in blood.

corral.def: 畜欄、圍住的人的人人物 (單數)
corral.ex: The horses were kept in a small corral.

correct.def: 正確的、改正、合適的人的人人物 (單數) (正)
correct.col: stand corrected, correct error, correct style
correct.ex: Please correct the local error in this page.

correction.def: 改正、糾正、懲罰、(市)下跌的人物 (單數) (糾)
correction.col: make a correction, house of correction
correction.ex: I need to make a small correction to file.

correctional.def: 矯正的人、教養的人的人物
correctional.ex: He was sent to a local correctional facility.

corrective.def: 糾正的、中和的人的人物 (單數) (糾)
corrective.ex: We must take immediate corrective measures.

correctly.ex: The work was done correctly and on time.

correlate.def: 相互關聯、使相關的人物、關聯的人的人物 (單數)
correlate.ex: Wealth doesn't always correlate with joy.

correlation.def: 相關、關聯的人物 (單數) (關)
correlation.ex: There is a direct correlation between data.

correlative.def: 關連的人、相關的人的人物 (單數)
correlative.ex: The study looks at the correlative effects.

correspond.def: 符合、對應、通信的人的人
correspond.ex: The two reports correspond to each other.

correspondence.def: 符合、一致、信件、通信的人物 (單數) (信)
correspondence.ex: She has kept all his private correspondence.

correspondent.def: 通訊員、記者、通信者人物 (單數) (記)
correspondent.ex: The foreign correspondent spoke from Japan.

corresponding.ex: Sales rose in the corresponding period today.

correspondingly.ex: Costs rose, and prices rose correspondingly.

corridor.col: long corridor, air corridor, power corridor
corridor.ex: He walked down the long and dark corridor.
